notes:
## Highlights

**Managed-hosting UX hardening sweep**

- **In-app server destroy** — Settings → General now lists every managed server on your account with one-click Copy URL + Destroy. Destroy is gated by an optimistic lock on the worker so concurrent attempts return a clean 409 instead of corrupting state, and the active server's keychain entry is cleared automatically.
- **Mode-switch resume marker** — local↔remote transfers now write a marker on every phase transition; on next launch a non-dismissable dialog detects an interrupted transfer and offers a "Discard attempt" path that re-aligns config with the booted-into mode.
- **Auth-failure banner** — backend rejecting your token (4401 WS close or `UNAUTHENTICATED` GraphQL extension) now surfaces a banner with three CTAs: Recover token, Update manually, or Work offline for now. Replaces the generic "disconnected" modal in remote mode.
- **Token-recovery polish** — recovery errors now surface the worker's `correlationId` so support can cross-reference D1 audit logs; the mint button shows an explicit loading state during the round-trip.

**Joint with keen-backend 0.10.0**

This release pairs with `keen-backend@0.10.0`, which ships the new `POST /api/recovery/mint-token` route used by the in-app token-recovery flow. The provisioning Worker version-gates `/recover-token` to backends ≥ 0.10.0, so older managed servers continue to work but won't offer recovery until they're rolled forward.

---

Full changelog: https://github.com/saputello2/keen-frontend/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0100---2026-04-25
